text:
is a video game released for the video game console. It was based on the animated series of the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series and the The End of Evangelion movie. The video game was only sold for 8800 Yen (plus tax) in Japan on 25 June 1999 by Bandai Entertainment.

Overview

This game is known for, in some ways, pushing the limits of the Nintendo 64's hardware, such as use of original footage from the show, as well as voice clips from the original series' cast. The game plays through most of the major battles from both the TV series and The End of Evangelion movie except for the battles with Gaghiel, Matarael, Ireul, Leliel and Armisael. Half of the levels are side-scrolling, 3D fighting levels, with the player controlling an Evangelion, while the other half involve their own styles of play.

The game has 14 missions, each focusing on one of the major enemies of the series.
Mission 1: Sachiel
Mission 2: Shamshel
Mission 3: Ramiel
Mission 4: Jet Alone
Mission 5: Israfel
Mission 6: Sandalphon
Mission 7: Sahaquiel
Mission 8: Bardiel
Mission 9: Zeruel
Mission 10: Arael
Mission 11: Tabris
Mission 12: Military Forces
Mission 12.5: MP Evas
Mission 13: MP Evas

Gameplay

 fighting Sachiel. For most of the game the player controls Unit-01.]]
The gameplay is mostly a fighting game between the Evas and Angels. There are three ways the player can play. The most common gameplays is Plain Fighting, where the player takes control of an Eva on a "3D-2D" Course, walking back and forth, attacking the enemy. The battles are mainly focused on pressing correct commands to activate cinematic attacks and grabs known as "Actions", while basics attacks are secondary. Another gameplay style used in the game is weponring. Various parts of the game include minigames as missions, where the player takes control of the Eva's weponring in order to accomplish a mission or objective. The final gameplay element used is free-for-all, where the player is still on a "3D-2D" Plain, but can move freely back and forth. This mode is used in missions 12, 12.5 and 13.

There are also modes outside the missions, such as a training mode named Simulation Mode. In this mode the player selects an Eva that has been unlocked in story mode and practices by shooting un-collared poly-models of different Angels, including ones that are not featured in the Story Mode, and receive a ranking based on how many Angel models are destroyed and how many shots are fired. It is possible to activate a Vs mode by using cheat codes, in which two players can select Evas with different weapons. The other unlockable modes are a sound test and Action Viewer, where the player can review all the Actions executed during the game.

Staff

Development: BEC Co. Ltd. 3rd Development Division.
Game Design: Kozo Igarashi, Yutaka Nagayama.
Main Programmer: Yutaka Nagayama.
Programmers: Shigeki Sugano, Hitoshi Saitou.
CG/3D Models: Yasuhiko Kikuchi, Takayuki Ōkubo, Kozo Igarashi.
CG/3D Motion: Yasuhiko Kikuchi, Takayuki Ōkubo, Kozo Igarashi.
2D Graphics Support: Peacock Ltd., Takeshi Ikenoya, Tomoki Oda.
Sound: Opus Corp., Jun Enoki.
Special Thanks: Hisaya Yabusaki, Shōji Takahashi, Atsushi Iwata, Shinji Usui, Yoshimitsu Watanabe, Yasuyuki Takahashi, Takenori Saitō, Yoshinori Obishaku, Yasumi Horiuchi, Isao Kaneki , Katsunori Kubo, Anime Sound, Audio Tanaka.
Voice Cast: Megumi Ogata, Kotono Mitsuishi, Yuriko Yamaguchi, Megumi Hayashibara, Yuko Miyamura, Fumihiko Tachiki, Motomu Kiyokawa, Hiro Yuki, Miki Nagasawa, Takehito Koyasu, Kōichi Yamadera, Tomokazu Seki, Akira Ishida, Mugihito.
Cover Illustration: Yoh Yoshinari
